conversation_log.summary: Revised acceptance criteria after task revision invocation. Original single criterion ('The <h1> heading on the /test page displays "test"') was not automatically testable — it required a running browser. Investigation of the codebase revealed that the /test route renders its heading via t("test.heading") (react-i18next), and the locale file apps/frontend/src/locales/en/common.json currently holds 'Connectivity Test for stack' for that key. Revised criteria are now fully file-checkable: (1) common.json test.heading equals exactly 'test', (2) no other key is removed or changed, (3) test.tsx is unchanged. Component must not be modified; only the locale JSON file changes — consistent with the established i18n convention confirmed by prior task #75.

Task is fully consistent with the i18n convention in arch/frontend.md: all user-facing strings must go through react-i18next, translation files live in src/locales/<lang>/common.json, and components must not hardcode strings. Only apps/frontend/src/locales/en/common.json changes — no structural, naming, or interface concerns.

All three acceptance criteria are testable as written. Criterion 1 (test.heading value equals exactly 'test') is a precise JSON key/value assertion — binary pass/fail. Criterion 2 (no other key removed or changed) is a full-file structural comparison against a known baseline — binary pass/fail. Criterion 3 (test.tsx unchanged) is a file-content identity check — binary pass/fail. No criterion is vague, subjective, or redundant with CI-enforced checks.
Implemented change to test.heading in apps/frontend/src/locales/en/common.json from 'Connectivity Test for stack' to 'test'. PR #90 opened.

decisions.approach: Update test.heading in apps/frontend/src/locales/en/common.json from 'Connectivity Test for stack' to 'test'. No component changes required.
